zh.homeIntro1: 赛博朋克（Cyberpunk）是1980年代兴起的科幻文学流派，以威廉·吉布森1984年出版的《神经漫游者》（Neuromancer）为开山之作。该流派描绘了一个高科技与低生活并存的近未来世界——跨国企业取代政府成为真正的权力中心，黑客在虚拟的赛博空间中穿行，人类与机器的界限日趋模糊。
zh.homeIntro2: 赛博朋克的核心作品包括吉布森的《神经漫游者》（1984）、尼尔·斯蒂芬森的《雪崩》（1992）、菲利普·K·迪克的《仿生人会梦见电子羊吗？》（1968，改编为电影《银翼杀手》）、以及士郎正宗的《攻壳机动队》（1989）等。这些作品在互联网尚未普及的年代，精准预见了数字时代的诸多面貌。
zh.homeIntro3: 从赛博空间到元宇宙，从黑客文化到加密货币，从企业霸权到监控资本主义——赛博朋克作家们在数十年前的想象，如今正逐一成为现实。本站整理了16条来自经典赛博朋克作品的预言性描述，并对其与现实的对应关系进行了验证分析。
zh.coreQuote: 「赛博空间。每天都有数十亿合法操作者经历的共识幻觉……从人类系统的每一台电脑中抽取的数据图形化呈现。」——威廉·吉布森《神经漫游者》（1984）
keyPointsList: 1968年 —— 菲利普·K·迪克《仿生人会梦见电子羊吗？》出版，预见人造人与身份危机
1982年 —— 电影《银翼杀手》上映，定义赛博朋克视觉美学
1984年 —— 威廉·吉布森《神经漫游者》出版，首创「赛博空间」概念
1989年 —— 士郎正宗《攻壳机动队》连载开始，探索人机融合与数字意识
1992年 —— 尼尔·斯蒂芬森《雪崩》出版，预见「元宇宙」与虚拟化身
sourceList: 预言内容摘编自赛博朋克经典文学作品原文及其公认解读（《神经漫游者》、《雪崩》、《仿生人会梦见电子羊吗？》、《攻壳机动队》）
验证评价基于公开新闻报道、行业数据和技术发展记录
编者观点不代表学术定论，赛博朋克作品为虚构文学而非预言
站点图标采用电路板眼睛符号——赛博朋克的核心意象是人机融合与数字化监控，电路与眼睛的结合体现了这一主题

en.homeIntro1: Cyberpunk is a science fiction genre that emerged in the 1980s, with William Gibson's Neuromancer (1984) as its founding work. The genre depicts a near-future world where high technology coexists with social decay — multinational corporations replace governments as true power centers, hackers traverse virtual cyberspace, and the boundary between human and machine grows ever thinner.
en.homeIntro2: Core cyberpunk works include Gibson's Neuromancer (1984), Neal Stephenson's Snow Crash (1992), Philip K. Dick's Do Androids Dream of Electric Sheep? (1968, adapted as Blade Runner), and Masamune Shirow's Ghost in the Shell (1989). These works accurately foresaw many aspects of the digital age before the internet was widely adopted.
en.homeIntro3: From cyberspace to the metaverse, from hacking culture to cryptocurrency, from corporate hegemony to surveillance capitalism — the visions of cyberpunk authors from decades ago are becoming reality one by one. This site compiles 16 prophetic descriptions from classic cyberpunk works and evaluates their correspondence with the real world.
en.coreQuote: "Cyberspace. A consensual hallucination experienced daily by billions of legitimate operators... A graphic representation of data abstracted from the banks of every computer in the human system." — William Gibson, Neuromancer (1984)
keyPointsList: 1968 —— Philip K. Dick publishes Do Androids Dream of Electric Sheep?, foreseeing androids and identity crisis
1982 —— Blade Runner released, defining cyberpunk visual aesthetics
1989 —— Masamune Shirow begins Ghost in the Shell, exploring human-machine fusion
1992 —— Neal Stephenson publishes Snow Crash, foreseeing the 'metaverse' and avatars
sourceList: Prophecies excerpted from classic cyberpunk literary works and their widely accepted interpretations (Neuromancer, Snow Crash, Do Androids Dream of Electric Sheep?, Ghost in the Shell)
Verification based on public news reports, industry data, and technology development records
Editorial opinions do not represent academic consensus; cyberpunk works are fiction, not prophecy
Site icon: circuit-board eye — cyberpunk's core imagery is human-machine fusion and digital surveillance, combining circuits with an eye to embody this theme
